query: is apelin level decreased in type 2 diabetes

retrieved_docs.title: Pathophysiology of Obesity -- Issues of Concern -- Role of Hormones in Obesity
retrieved_docs.content: Several studies suggest adipose tissue can collectively secrete more than 50 hormones and signaling molecules termed adipokines. These adipokines play a vital role in immunity and glucose metabolism. [30] The adipose tissue of a lean individual secretes anti-inflammatory adipokines such as transforming growth factor-beta (TGF-beta), interleukins (IL)-10, IL-4, IL-13, IL-1 receptor antagonist (IL-1Ra), adiponectin, and apelin. In contrast, the adipose tissue of an obese individual secretes mainly pro-inflammatory cytokines such as TNFs, IL-6, resistin, visfatin, leptin, angiotensin II, and plasminogen activator inhibitor-1. [31] Leptin, a hormone that plays a role in appetite and energy balance regulation, along with pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-alpha), are secreted by adipose tissue cells. [32] Leptin is secreted in proportion to the amount of fat stored in adipose tissue. Another hormone secreted by adipose tissue is adiponectin, which decreases in proportion to fat storage in the body. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Pancreas -- Clinical Significance -- Type 2 DM
retrieved_docs.content: Pathophysiology: Beta-cell dysfunction manifests in different ways: (1) reductions in insulin release, (2) changes in pulsatile insulin secretion, (3) an abnormality in the efficiency of proinsulin to insulin conversion, and (4) reduces the release of amylin. Insulin resistance is present in most patients with T2D. Insulin resistance is characterized by higher than expected plasma glucose level with the prevailing plasma insulin secretion. In patients with T2D insulin, stimulation fails to induce normal GLUT4 protein translocation to the sarcolemma in skeletal muscle membrane. Also, excessive production of free fatty acids and overexpression of TNF-alpha by adipocytes have been proposed as mechanisms for the development of insulin resistance. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity Brain Gut Adipocyte Interaction -- Function -- Role of the adipose tissue in energy homeostasis:
retrieved_docs.content: Adipose tissues balance energy and regulate long term body weight. They communicate with the brain through adipokine, which includes leptin, adiponectin, apelin, and resistin and neuronal inputs. Brown adipose tissue is involved in energy balance. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Atrial Natriuretic Peptide -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Again, ANP is not routinely measured. However, in the setting of BNP and NT-proBNP testing, comorbid conditions such as anemia, Type 2 diabetes, kidney disease, obesity, and acute coronary syndrome can affect the levels. [22] Levels are lower in obese patients and tend to be higher in the setting of kidney disease, acute coronary syndrome, and diabetes. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Type 2 Diabetes -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: T2DM is an insulin-resistance condition with associated beta-cell dysfunction. Initially, there is a compensatory increase in insulin secretion, which maintains glucose levels in the normal range. As the disease progresses, beta cells change, and insulin secretion is unable to maintain glucose homeostasis, producing hyperglycemia. Most of the patients with T2DM are obese or have a higher body fat percentage, distributed predominantly in the abdominal region. This adipose tissue itself promotes insulin resistance through various inflammatory mechanisms, including increased FFA release and adipokine dysregulation. Lack of physical activity, prior GDM in those with hypertension or dyslipidemia also increases the risk of developing T2DM. Evolving data suggest a role for adipokine dysregulation, inflammation, abnormal incretin biology with decreased incretins such as glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-I) or incretin resistance, hyperglucagonemia, increased renal glucose reabsorption, and abnormalities in gut microbiota.

retrieved_docs.content: Important trials: SCALE-Sleep apnea, SCALE-diabetes, SCALE-obesity and prediabetes, SCALE-maintenance, LEADER

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Adiponectin -- Clinical Significance -- Insulin Resistance
retrieved_docs.content: Studies involving rodent models have demonstrated the role of adiponectin in promoting insulin sensitization. Moreover, numerous positive correlations between insulin resistance and hypoadiponectinemia have been established in humans. Hypoadiponectinemia is a feature in pathologies such as gestational diabetes, type 2 diabetes, and diabetes associated with lipodystrophy. Further, low adiponectin levels have been demonstrated in patients with insulin resistance regardless of obesity.

retrieved_docs.title: Obesity Brain Gut Adipocyte Interaction -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Obesity refers to abnormal or excess body weight (mainly fat) concerning height. It is a growing health problem in the United States as well as around the globe with a significant economic burden. More than a third of individuals in the USA have a body mass index (BMI) of over 30kg/m^2, and the cost for obesity has risen to $147 billion annually.  It is also associated with various comorbidities, which include type 2 diabetes mellitus, heart diseases, stroke, obstructive sleep apnea, Hypogonadism, and premature death. According to the Center for Disease Control and Prevention (2015-2016 data), approximately 93.3 million US adults were obese. [1] [2] A similar trend is on the rise in many other western countries.

retrieved_docs.title: Dawn Phenomenon -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The importance of recognition and control of the dawn phenomenon in type 2 diabetes lies in preventing additional exposure to elevated blood glucose levels, thereby preventing increasing insulin resistance. The increasing defect in insulin secretion and sensitivity has been shown to produce a steady decline in the quality of normal glucose metabolism over the life course of the disease. [15] Additional data from Monnier et al., has indicated that the dawn phenomenon could affect the overall glycemic control in type 2 diabetes, elevating the HbA1c levels by as much as 0.4%. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Arginine Vasopressin Disorder (Diabetes Insipidus) -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: AVP-D is secondary to inadequate or impaired secretion of AVP from the posterior pituitary gland in response to osmotic stimulation and a decrease in blood pressure. AVP is synthesized as a precursor complex in the supraoptic and periventricular nuclei of the hypothalamus and encoded by the AVP-neurophysin II gene. It is released by calcium-dependent exocytosis. The osmoreceptors are found in the hypothalamus, arterial baroreceptors, and the atrial stretch receptors. In many cases, the neurohypophysis is destroyed by various acquired or congenital anatomic lesions secondary to pressure or infiltration. The resulting hypotonic diuresis depends on the degree of destruction of the neurohypophysis, leading to complete or partial deficiency of AVP secretion.

retrieved_docs.title: Natriuretic Peptide B Type Test -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: BNP and NT-proBNP levels may be affected by specific comorbidities, such as chronic renal failure, type 2 diabetes, obesity, and acute coronary syndrome. [23] Levels are higher in patients with renal failure, diabetes mellitus, and acute coronary syndrome, and lower in patients with obesity. [7] Decreasing renal function with age may be partly responsible for the increase in BNP/NT-proBNP. In the absence of heart failure, the concentration of BNP/NT-proBNP increases in patients with a decreasing glomerular filtration rate. [35] Older individuals who appear healthy may have declining cardiac function not detectable by current noninvasive techniques. [36]

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Bombesin -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Loss of bombesin receptors correlates with age-dependent obesity, hypertension, glucose intolerance, and high insulin levels. This expanded adipose deposition may, in part, be due to a decline in energy consumption without a shift in eating or movement, which infers that bombesin receptors may signify a plausible target upon which notable advancement can take place in the realm of anti-obesity agents.

retrieved_docs.content: Dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitors prolong the activity of endogenous GLP-1 and GIP by preventing their breakdown. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Atrial Natriuretic Peptide -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: As it pertains to lipid metabolism, as described above, this is an area of ongoing research. The discoveries that the natriuretic peptides play a role in lipid mobilization within human white adipose tissue, skeletal muscle fat oxidation, and dissipation of energy in brown adipose tissue all point to the fact that the natriuretic peptides are possibly associated with glucose homeostasis, fatty acid metabolism, and insulin sensitivity. [6] [7] [35] [7] [6] [36] It has been hypothesized that there may even be an associated predictive value in the development of diabetes. [37] Given that obesity is currently a huge health problem, this could also represent a future area for therapeutic intervention if a peptide in similar composition to ANP but with a longer half-life is formulated.

retrieved_docs.title: Dawn Phenomenon -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Epidemiological analyses have provided information that a 1% increase in A1c can be associated with a 15%-20% increased risk of cardiovascular complications. [16] Additionally, a 2012 study reported by researchers from Sweden indicated that a 0.8% reduction in A1c could produce a cardiovascular death risk reduction as high as 45%. [17] Controlling the dawn phenomenon alone could achieve as much as half the A1c improvement needed for this benefit, and since it is felt to be one of the earliest disorders in the natural progression of type 2 diabetes, it should be taken into consideration as an indicator for more aggressive therapy early in the disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1903
retrieved_docs.content: Glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) analogues are classified as members of the exenatid family, indicating that they share the structural and pharmacologic characteristics of exenatid compounds, which function as GLP‑1 receptor agonists commonly used in the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 2063
retrieved_docs.content: Glucagon-like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) analogues constitute a class of therapeutic agents that mimic the incretin hormone GLP‑1, enhancing glucose‑dependent insulin secretion, inhibiting glucagon release, slowing gastric emptying, and promoting satiety. Liraglutide is a member of this GLP‑1 analogue class, and its pharmacologic actions make it valuable for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus and for weight management in patients with obesity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 1478
retrieved_docs.content: Glibenklamid is identified as a member of the class of Sulfonylureas for blood glucose lowering, indicating that it belongs to this group of oral antidiabetic agents that reduce plasma glucose levels by stimulating insulin secretion from the pancreas. As a sulfonylurea, glibenklamid plays a role in therapeutic strategies for managing hyperglycemia in patients with type 2 diabetes, underscoring its importance in achieving effective glycemic control.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1919
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism include the SMQ titled “ارتفاع السكر في الدم/داء السكري البادِئُ الجديد” (new‑onset diabetes mellitus with elevated blood glucose), and this SMQ in turn comprises the clinical effect “تأثير إدرار البول” (increased urine output). This relationship indicates that new‑onset diabetes, classified within lipid metabolism disorders, is associated with the manifestation of polyuria, highlighting the interconnected nature of metabolic dysregulation and its renal‑related symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2479
retrieved_docs.content: Disease condition determination, uncontrolled is associated with diabetes mellitus with complications, indicating that an uncontrolled disease state is linked to the presence of diabetes mellitus that has progressed to a complicated form, underscoring the importance of effective disease management to prevent or mitigate diabetic complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 5 -- Community 2962
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney failure (فشل كلوي حاد) is clinically associated with type 2 diabetes mellitus (مرض السكري نوع 2) and with reduced blood volume (قلة حجم الدم). Type 2 diabetes can predispose patients to acute kidney injury through chronic hyperglycemia‑induced vascular and tubular damage, making the kidneys more vulnerable to hemodynamic stress. Conversely, hypovolemia decreases renal perfusion and can directly precipitate acute kidney failure. Recognizing that both type 2 diabetes and low intravascular volume are important clinical contributors to acute kidney failure guides clinicians to monitor diabetic patients closely, ensure adequate hydration, and intervene promptly to preserve renal function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1423
retrieved_docs.content: Dipeptidyl peptidase 4 (DPP‑4) inhibitors for blood glucose lowering constitute a therapeutic class of oral antihyperglycemic agents, and both alogliptin and sitagliptin are members of this class.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2721
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, اعتلال عصبي سكري, فرط أنسولين الدم, and ورم غلوكاغوني. ارتفاع السكر في الدم (high blood sugar) is a primary manifestation of diabetes, reflecting impaired glucose regulation by the endocrine pancreas. اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy) is a common chronic complication of prolonged hyperglycemia, affecting peripheral nerves and leading to sensory and motor deficits. فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia) denotes excessive insulin secretion, which can occur in early insulin resistance or insulin‑producing tumors and may precede or accompany diabetes. ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma) is a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, often resulting in persistent hyperglycemia and weight loss. Recognizing how these conditions are interrelated within endocrine pancreatic disease supports comprehensive diagnosis, monitoring, and therapeutic strategies for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2044
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated plasma osmolality is clinically associated with several forms of diabetes, including type 1 diabetes, type 2 diabetes, and insulin‑requiring type 2 diabetes. In addition to its relationship with these diabetic conditions, elevated plasma osmolality frequently co‑occurs with low blood volume, suggesting that reduced circulatory volume may contribute to or exacerbate the increase in plasma osmolality observed in diabetic patients. Recognizing the link between elevated plasma osmolality, various diabetes subtypes, and low blood volume can aid clinicians in monitoring fluid and electrolyte balance, optimizing glycemic management, and preventing complications related to dehydration and hyperosmolar states.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1932
retrieved_docs.content: Acute kidney failure (فشل كلوي حاد) is clinically associated with low blood volume (قلة حجم الدم) and with insulin‑requiring type 2 diabetes mellitus (مرض السكري من النوع الثاني (2) المتطلب للأنسولين). In addition, acute kidney failure has a sub‑entity of extrarenal nitrogen accumulation (نيتروجينية الدم خارج الكلوية), indicating that nitrogenous waste may build up outside the kidneys. These interconnections emphasize that hypovolemia and poorly controlled insulin‑dependent type 2 diabetes are important risk factors or co‑morbid conditions for acute kidney failure, and that monitoring extrarenal nitrogen levels is relevant for its diagnosis and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2310
retrieved_docs.content: Lixisenatide is classified as a glucagon‑like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) analogue, belonging to the therapeutic class of GLP‑1 analogues that mimic the incretin hormone GLP‑1 to enhance glucose‑dependent insulin secretion, suppress glucagon release, and slow gastric emptying. As a member of this drug class, lixisenatide is employed in the management of type 2 diabetes mellitus, often in combination with other antidiabetic agents, and its pharmacologic actions are consistent with those of other GLP‑1 analogues.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1898
retrieved_docs.content: Metformin is classified as a member of the biguanide blood‑glucose‑lowering drug class, which comprises agents used to reduce blood glucose levels.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 197
retrieved_docs.content: Insulin aspart (human) exerts a physiologic effect of decreased lipolysis and acts on the INSR protein (human) as a molecular target. This rapid‑acting insulin analogue is employed in the treatment of both مرض السكري نوع 1 (type 1 diabetes) and مرض السكري نوع 2 (type 2 diabetes). Clinical observations link both نوع 1 and نوع 2 diabetes to an increased risk of فشل كلوي حاد (acute kidney failure) and to ارتفاع أسمولالية البلازما (elevated plasma osmolality). Acute kidney failure frequently co‑occurs with dehydration (التجفاف), abnormal mental states (حالة نفسية غير طبيعية), low blood volume (قلة حجم الدم), and is especially associated with insulin‑requiring type 2 diabetes (مرض السكري من النوع الثاني (2) المتطلب للأنسولين). Elevated plasma osmolality is also clinically associated with insulin‑requiring type 2 diabetes and abnormal mental states, and it commonly co‑occurs with low blood volume. Dehydration is a physiological precursor that maps to elevated plasma osmolality, reflecting the close relationship between fluid loss and osmotic imbalance. Both فشل كلوي حاد and ارتفاع أسمولالية البلازما are categorized under اضطراب السبيل البولي (urinary tract disorder), indicating that they are specific manifestations within the broader spectrum of urinary tract pathology. Together, these interconnections highlight how insulin therapy, diabetes, renal dysfunction, fluid‑electrolyte disturbances, and neuro‑psychiatric manifestations are intertwined, underscoring the importance of integrated assessment and management in patients with diabetes‑related complications.
